response.justification: Bogdan is a strong, versatile software engineer with genuine staff-level seniority and a broad polyglot background, but the role demands 8+ years of production AI delivery and deep expertise in the GenAI stack (RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation, multi-agent frameworks). His hands-on AI work is limited to roughly one month at re:cinq (April 2025), where he contributes to MCP tooling and agentic workflows — encouraging early signal, but nowhere near the required depth or tenure. Python is absent from his skills, cloud experience is surface-level (AWS CDK at Optilyz), and there is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or customer-facing technical leadership in an AI consulting context.
strengths: MCP (Model Context Protocol) work at re:cinq directly maps to a key technology listed in the JD, showing early alignment with the agentic-workflow space.
Demonstrated staff-level leadership across multiple organizations, including workshops, conference talks, and mentoring — consistent with the role's technical-leadership expectations.
Long record of shipping production systems at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby; millions of direct-mail items at Optilyz), proving operational maturity.
concerns: No Python listed anywhere in skills or work history — a primary language for the role — and the only language gap that would require significant ramp-up before contributing.
AI/GenAI experience is confined to the current role started in April 2025; the JD requires 8+ years of AI solution delivery, leaving roughly a 7.9-year deficit against the stated minimum.
No evidence of RAG pipelines, vector databases, LLM evaluation frameworks, or multi-agent orchestration (LangGraph, CrewAI), which are explicit required and preferred qualifications.
